quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: MRM must be justified case-by-case; suited to strategic behavior: express lanes and pricing first-listed
technical_implication: pick use cases where route/mode/price response binds
our_design_response: I-66 managed-lane pricing = Guidebook's first-listed justification; use_case in subarea_run.yml
notes: Guidebook also warns MRM NOT justified for uncongested networks -> D-6 window rule

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: modelers must prove cost-effectiveness before starting; want parametric cost estimate (zones lane-miles periods scenarios)
technical_implication: LOE must be predictable from network parameters
our_design_response: FR-15 parametric LOE estimator in intake gate + measured time ledger (README)
notes: ledger live; FR-15 not yet built

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: larger meso scope means significant extra data collection (signals travel time counts)
technical_implication: data plan must precede modeling
our_design_response: DATA_SOURCES.md = FHWA Table-7-style source table with states; intake gate blocks undeclared conventions

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: increasing interest in probe data for OD and path estimation but sample sizes thin at high resolution
technical_implication: probe/TMC data are calibration inputs not decoration
our_design_response: TMC crosswalk + FR-7 speed profiles + G7 with/without-measurements convention
notes: blocked on congested-window RITIS pull (Loop A)

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: lack of standard formats + manual conversion utilities cause errors and eat calibration time
technical_implication: conversion must be automated with QA
our_design_response: OMX->per-mode converter with PROVEN zone mapping (max diff 0.0) + refuses silent demand loss; dtalite_qa validate/intake
notes: mapping proof: OMX index = MWCOG TAZ id

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: streamlined regionwide-static-OD -> time-dependent subarea OD/path flow critically needed (verbatim)
technical_implication: this IS the missing product
our_design_response: FR-9/FR-19 DTAT trajectory export + gateway OD; G6 exactness proof

gap_source: ZONE audit (this project) + SOPAGA consistency theme
quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: multiple zone layers (TAZ/external stations/special centroids/superzones/gates) corrupt demand silently if mixed
technical_implication: every layer needs a registered mapping
our_design_response: ZONE_LAYER_REGISTRY.md L1-L8 + protect rule for external stations + id_bridge registry
notes: external-station exact set pending MWCOG doc

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: regional-to-subarea refinement cut corridor analysis from weeks/months to days
technical_implication: fast reusable subarea extraction is the headline benefit
our_design_response: prepare 1.6s + compact 16s + fast-response 65s measured; blueprint folder reuse
notes: source attribution via XZ summary of ch.4

gap_source: the 2006 TRB subarea-OD study authors TRB'06
quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: subarea OD must preserve through demand via induced OD from path assignment then measurement update
technical_implication: two-stage structure
our_design_response: FOCUSING classify + DTAT induced OD (stage 1) + ODME (stage 2) + G8 gateway agreement

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: consistency/convergence checking across resolutions weak; vendors provide little automation
technical_implication: convergence + cross-resolution agreement must be gated not eyeballed
our_design_response: S5 mini-steps: gap monitor + screening label + leak gate + G6/G8; runbook cross-loop invalidation

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: run times of hours-days per scenario block adoption
technical_implication: scenario cost must drop orders of magnitude
our_design_response: superzone compact (FR-18) + FREEZE/REPLAY + 65s/scenario measured; M-3 streaming queued

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: validation usually qualitative; must be quantitative and defensible
technical_implication: adopt agency-official thresholds
our_design_response: TOSAM gates (15/20% vol; 20/30% TT; >=85%) + CBI targets (FR-16) + scorecard (FR-12)
notes: Loop A blocked on RITIS window

gap_source: SOPAGA ch.8 Behavioral Response + 2013 AMS Hub
quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: pricing/HOV/traveler-info response is the weakest modeling area
technical_implication: class-specific demand + toll response required
our_design_response: FR-17 class-split (Express_Toll_Eligible) + VTRC logit constants + E1 HOV-exempt scenario; AM smoke showed no-HOV-exempt baseline suppresses EB inside (evidence)

gap_source: SOPAGA ch.8 + 2013 AMS Hub trajectory-based MOEs
quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: tools should read vehicle trajectories and estimate mobility/safety/environment consistently
technical_implication: trajectory (not route columns) is the downstream contract
our_design_response: FR-19 v2 DTAT trajectory/event export (user correction adopted); Q9 eco/emission fields reserved

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: config parameters differ by tool/theory/corridor; common fields + tool-specific extensions needed
technical_implication: config must be extensible not hard-coded
our_design_response: subarea_run.yml holds policies (HOV/toll/VOT/VOR/BHT/eco) as optional schema fields; kernel policy-blind (R9)

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: training gap: demand modelers weak in flow/simulation; simulation modelers weak in assignment/DTA/choice
technical_implication: stovepiping between modeler tribes breaks integration
our_design_response: gate-based review with per-stage contracts/artifacts + runbook readable by both tribes + per-step tool-substitution notes
notes: names per XZ summary; not in my SOPAGA footnote extraction

expert_or_agency: WSDOT (WSDOT staff/WSDOT staff) + VDOT contractors (VDOT contractor staff/VDOT contractor staff/VDOT contractor staff)
quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: agencies have TDM and micro SOPs but none for meso/DTA/MRM
technical_implication: an SOP must fall out of the project
our_design_response: I-66_ams per-step READMEs + WORKFLOW_RUNBOOK = the SOP; publication path to public repo

gap_source: SOPAGA ch.7 Few Success Stories (a university PI/UC-Irvine)
quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: one well-documented pilot became the template for dozens of follow-on projects
technical_implication: the blueprint effect is the adoption mechanism
our_design_response: I-66_ams = the blueprint folder; copy-swap-rerun for next corridor; STREAMLINED_PROCESS blueprint rule

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: hybrid/MRM hard to sell without concrete demonstrations
technical_implication: sponsor-facing evidence package required
our_design_response: GUI4GMNS dashboard + before/after CBI + head-to-head vs TransModeler + time ledger

gap_source: SOPAGA ch.8 Model Archiving (WSDOT staff/WSDOT staff WSDOT; NDOT staff NDOT; VDOT contractor staff et al. VDOT)
quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: meso/micro models are one-offs; build-once-use-many; archive project enhancements BACK INTO the master model
technical_implication: versioned master + project delta archive
our_design_response: every I-66 artifact (bridges/coding/TMC maps/CBI rules) registered back into NVTA-GMNS master via id_bridge + manifests; run-from-manifest reproducibility

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: ABM-DTA integration with core areas coded in higher detail; subarea extraction from regional DTA
technical_implication: regional-to-subarea with variable resolution is proven practice
our_design_response: FOCUSING tiers = variable resolution by measured significance; edge-model observation (SOPAGA) embraced

quoted_or_paraphrased_concern: practitioners deliberately over-detail coarse models before export (practice ahead of research)
technical_implication: treat the 135-column NVTA link.csv as a feature
our_design_response: network profile (FR-3) preserves all attribute richness through conversion
